title: Baker Hughes data show a decline in the active U.S. oil-drilling rig count
text: Baker Hughes BKR, +1.06% on Friday reported that the number of active U.S. rigs drilling for oil fell by 5 to 520 this week. The total active U.S. rig count, which includes those drilling for natural gas, fell 12 to stand at 642, according to Baker Hughes.

title: Baker Hughes: The LNG Boom Can Be A Catalyst For This Stock
text: Investment in liquefied natural gas has greatly stepped up since 2022 when Europe lost its access to piped gas from Russia. As a supplier of gas turbines and other related equipment, Baker Hughes is positioned to benefit from the LNG trend. Baker Hughes' oilfield service business is also benefiting from a renaissance in upstream capex, particularly internationally and offshore.

title: US drillers cut oil and gas rigs for fifth week in a row - Baker Hughes
text: U.S. energy firms this week cut the number of oil and natural gas rigs operating for a fifth week in a row, energy services firm Baker Hughes said in its closely followed report on Friday.

title: Oil & Gas Are Moving In August, Here Are The 3 Industry Favorites
text: Whether it is the forces of commodity cycles, the changing tides of a world economy, or even straight manipulation from the most prominent players who hear whispers from OPEC's next decision, the only certainty is that oil and gas prices are swinging hard. Crude oil has swung from below $60 per barrel in the last quarter to above $80 per barrel as of today's new quarter, and natural gas has followed a similar path.

title: Baker Hughes and Avports Sign Agreement to Develop Lower-carbon Energy Solutions for Airport Industry
text: HOUSTON and DULLES, Va., Aug. 08, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Baker Hughes (NASDAQ: BKR), an energy technology company, announced Tuesday a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with airport management and operations company Avports to develop, implement and operate onsite microgrid solutions for the airport industry. The collaboration agreement addresses emissions reduction and the global industry's goal to focus on zero-emission buildings, horizontal airport infrastructure, vehicles and aircraft systems.

title: Halliburton, Schlumberger, Baker Hughes: Time to buy oil services stocks?
text: Halliburton (HAL) According to recent analysis by UBS, Halliburton Company (NYSE:HAL) beat adjusted earnings per share (EPS) estimates in Q2 2023, reporting $0.77 per share against the investment bank's estimate of $0.73 and the Street's $0.75.
